summary: Salivary cortisol does not have sufficient validity or specificity to serve as a reliable indicator of pain in neonates because its elevation results from general stress responses, not specific pain stimulation. Validated behavioral assessments remain superior for evaluating infant pain compared to biological markers like saliva-based hormones.
